seo_description: Current time in Abidjan, Côte d'Ivoire. Live GMT clock — UTC+0 year-round, no Daylight Saving Time. Abidjan time vs London, Paris, Lagos, Dakar.

faq.question: What time is it in Abidjan right now?
faq.answer: Abidjan, Ivory Coast (Côte d'Ivoire) uses Greenwich Mean Time (GMT, UTC+0) year-round. Ivory Coast does not observe Daylight Saving Time. The IANA identifier is Africa/Abidjan. Abidjan operates on Greenwich Mean Time (GMT), which is UTC+0 year-round. Côte d'Ivoire does not observe Daylight Saving Time, so the clock never changes. Use the live clock above to see the exact current time in Abidjan with live seconds.

faq.question: What time zone is Abidjan in?
faq.answer: Abidjan is in the GMT (Greenwich Mean Time) zone, fixed at UTC+0 throughout the entire year. This is the same offset as London in winter, Dakar (Senegal), Monrovia (Liberia), and Conakry (Guinea). Abidjan is 1 hour behind Lagos (Nigeria), which uses West Africa Time (WAT/UTC+1), and 5 hours ahead of New York during Eastern Standard Time.

faq.question: Does Abidjan observe Daylight Saving Time?
faq.answer: No. Côte d'Ivoire does not observe Daylight Saving Time and never has. Abidjan remains at UTC+0 permanently. This means that while London is on GMT (UTC+0) in winter, the two cities share the same time — but when London switches to BST (UTC+1) in summer, London becomes 1 hour ahead of Abidjan.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Abidjan and London?
faq.answer: Abidjan and London share the same time during winter, when London is on GMT (UTC+0). From late March to late October, London switches to British Summer Time (BST/UTC+1), making London 1 hour ahead of Abidjan. When it is 3:00 PM in London in July, it is 2:00 PM in Abidjan.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Abidjan and Paris?
faq.answer: Abidjan is 1 hour behind Paris during winter (CET/UTC+1) and 2 hours behind during summer (CEST/UTC+2). Despite France's historical ties to Côte d'Ivoire, the two cities are on different time zones. When it is 12:00 PM in Paris in January, it is 11:00 AM in Abidjan. In summer, noon in Paris corresponds to 10:00 AM in Abidjan.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Abidjan and Lagos?
faq.answer: Abidjan is 1 hour behind Lagos, Nigeria. Lagos uses West Africa Time (WAT/UTC+1) year-round, while Abidjan uses GMT (UTC+0). This permanent 1-hour difference reflects the different colonial timezone inheritances of anglophone and francophone West Africa. When it is 9:00 AM in Lagos, it is 8:00 AM in Abidjan.

faq.question: What is the best time to call Abidjan from the US?
faq.answer: From New York (EST/UTC-5), Abidjan is 5 hours ahead in winter and 4 hours ahead in summer (EDT). To reach Abidjan during business hours (8:00 AM – 5:00 PM GMT), New York callers should dial between 3:00 AM – 12:00 PM Eastern in winter. The most practical overlap is an early New York morning call — 8:00 AM Eastern in winter reaches Abidjan at 1:00 PM, well within business hours.

faq.question: What are business hours in Abidjan?
faq.answer: Standard business hours in Abidjan run 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM or 8:00 AM – 6:00 PM GMT (UTC+0), Monday through Friday. Government offices typically close at 3:30 PM or 4:00 PM. The Abidjan Stock Exchange (BRVM), which serves the broader UEMOA West African economic zone, operates from 9:00 AM – 3:00 PM GMT. Abidjan's large French-speaking business community follows continental European business culture adapted for the West African context.

content_blocks.title: Abidjan Time Zone — Permanent GMT UTC+0, No DST
content_blocks.content: Abidjan, the economic capital of Côte d'Ivoire (Ivory Coast) and the largest city in Francophone Africa, operates on Greenwich Mean Time (GMT/UTC+0) permanently. Côte d'Ivoire has never observed Daylight Saving Time, placing Abidjan on a stable, fixed UTC+0 offset year-round. This aligns Abidjan with London in winter (when London is also on GMT), and with Dakar (Senegal), Monrovia (Liberia), and Conakry (Guinea) at all times. Notably, Abidjan is 1 hour behind Lagos (Nigeria), despite being neighbouring major West African cities — reflecting the legacy of British vs. French colonial timezone choices. When London shifts to BST (UTC+1) in summer, London becomes 1 hour ahead of Abidjan. The permanent UTC+0 offset makes Abidjan highly compatible with London-based and international financial institutions.

content_blocks.title: Abidjan — West Africa's Financial Capital and Cocoa Hub
content_blocks.content: Abidjan is one of Africa's most dynamic commercial cities and the undisputed financial capital of Francophone West Africa. Côte d'Ivoire produces approximately 40% of the world's cocoa supply, and Abidjan's Port Autonome d'Abidjan is one of the continent's busiest ports, handling cocoa, coffee, palm oil, rubber, and petroleum products. The city hosts the Bourse Régionale des Valeurs Mobilières (BRVM), the regional stock exchange shared by eight UEMOA member states. International banks, multinational companies, and development institutions have established regional headquarters in Abidjan's Plateau business district, drawn by its infrastructure, stable financial environment, and strategic West African position. French is the language of business, education, and government. Abidjan's population of over 5 million makes it one of Africa's largest cities.

content_blocks.title: Scheduling Calls and Business Meetings with Abidjan
content_blocks.content: Abidjan's permanent GMT (UTC+0) offset creates excellent alignment with European business hubs in winter. From London (GMT in winter), there is zero time difference — London business hours perfectly overlap with Abidjan. In summer, London advances to BST (UTC+1), placing Abidjan 1 hour behind. From Paris (CET/UTC+1), Abidjan is 1 hour behind in winter; from Paris in summer (CEST/UTC+2), Abidjan is 2 hours behind, meaning Paris morning meetings should be scheduled for 10:00 AM or later to catch Abidjan at 8:00 AM. From New York (EST/UTC-5), the 5-hour gap in winter means 8:00 AM Eastern corresponds to 1:00 PM Abidjan — a comfortable early-afternoon slot for West Africa. From Dubai (UTC+4), Abidjan is 4 hours behind — Dubai afternoon meetings align with Abidjan mornings. Côte d'Ivoire's country code is +225; Abidjan uses city codes 20, 21, 22, and 23.

time_difference_table.difference: Same time in winter; 1 hour behind London when London is on BST
1 hour behind Paris (2 hours behind when Paris is on CEST)
5 hours ahead of New York (4 hours when NY is on EDT)
1 hour behind Berlin (2 hours behind when Berlin is on CEST)
8 hours ahead (7 hours when LA is on PDT)
5 hours ahead (4 hours when Toronto is on EDT)
